item.title: Iran strikes vessel in Strait of Hormuz amid debate over "transit fees"
item.description: The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps struck a cargo ship near Oman on Thursday, further complicating the Iran-U.S. negotiations.

item.title: Trump's obsession with SAVE America Act drives Congress into standoff
item.description: The president and his conservative allies have stymied other legislation as they unsuccessfully try to pass a voting regulations bill that lacks even simple majority support in the Senate.

item.title: 6-alarm fire destroys old industrial building in Allentown, Pennsylvania
item.description: A massive fire in Allentown, Pennsylvania, forced nearby residents to evacuate their homes Wednesday night.

item.title: Powerful earthquakes strike Venezuela, hundreds dead or injured
item.description: Venezuela's acting president said the death toll from powerful twin earthquakes was likely to rise, as USGS modeling suggested thousands may have been killed.

item.title: Israel denies claim of pullback from southern Lebanon
item.description: Israel is denying a Reuters report that Israel had pulled some of its troops back from southern Lebanon. CBS News senior foreign correspondent Holly Williams reports.

item.title: Cassidy says he changed vote on Iran war powers after White House briefing
item.description: Republican Sen. Bill Cassidy of Louisiana says he decided not to continue supporting the Democrat-led effort to restrict President Trump's war powers in Iran after receiving a briefing at the White House. CBS News' Natalie Brand has more.

item.title: Early details on deadly back-to-back Venezuela earthquakes
item.description: At least 164 people were killed when two 7+ magnitude earthquakes struck Venezuela Thursday night. The death toll is likely to rise as crews race to access devastated areas. CBS News' Cristian Benavides reports on the damage and Ross Stein, lecturer in geophysics at Stanford University, joins with analysis.

item.title: Venezuela quake survivors describe devastation: "Everything collapsed"
item.description: A Caracas resident told CBS News that he "started to pray" when he felt the first earthquake hit Venezuela.

item.title: Big Tech is all in on AI. Now all they need is customers.
item.description: Technology companies are betting trillions of dollars that consumers will open their wallets for AI services. But what if Big Tech is wrong?

item.title: Supreme Court rules for Trump on 2 major immigration cases
item.description: The Supreme Court handed down rulings on two major immigration cases Thursday. One paves the way for the Trump administration to revive a restrictive immigration policy on asylum, while the other lets the president strip deportation protections from Syrians and Haitians. CBS News legal contributor Jessica Levinson has a breakdown of the decisions.

item.title: Influencer faces possible execution in Dubai after allegedly fatally stabbing man
item.description: U.K. influencer Brooke George says a man she met online repeatedly assaulted her in the UAE, according to an advocacy group. Now she's facing possible execution for allegedly stabbing him to death.

item.title: Apple hikes Mac and iPad prices amid soaring chip costs
item.description: The price hikes affected multiple Apple products, including the MacBook Neo, which increased from $599 to $699.

item.title: Watch: Markwayne Mullin gets heated over questions on Trump immigration policies involving children
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin screamed at Connecticut Democratic Rep. Rosa DeLauro during a House panel hearing over the Trump administration's immigration policies, including the separation of families during detention and deportation proceedings.

item.title: Reflecting Pool liner was cut with knife or razor, parks official says
item.description: National Park Service official Frank Lands also said at least 70 fence post tops were thrown in to the Reflecting Pool.

item.title: Venezuela earthquakes: Search for survivors continues as death toll soars
item.description: A frantic search for survivors is underway in Venezuela after back-to-back earthquakes Wednesday night in Venezuela. CBS News' Lilia Luciano reports and research geologist Kate Scharer joins with analysis.

item.title: DeSantis confirms "Alligator Alcatraz" shutting down operations
item.description: Conditions at "Alligator Alcatraz" were criticized by lawyers, families and human rights groups who claimed detainees were mistreated.

item.title: How much interest can a $20,000 long-term CD account earn if opened now?
item.description: If you want to protect and grow your $20,000 for an extended period, a long-term CD account is worth consideration.

item.title: USMNT should "change all ten players" in World Cup match against Turkey, DaMarcus Beasley says
item.description: The U.S. Men's National Soccer team will take on Turkey in its final World Cup group match before they head to the knockout round. Former USMNT player DaMarcus Beasley shares his advice for the team.

item.title: Taylor Swift rents Madison Square Garden, reigniting wedding venue rumors, New York Times report
item.description: The New York Times is reporting that Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce might tie the knot at Madison Square Garden. She has reportedly booked out the arena for the Fourth of July weekend, the rumored wedding date.

item.title: Supreme Court sides with Monsanto in case over cancer risks from weedkiller
item.description: The Supreme Court ruled that Monsanto cannot be held liable under state laws for failing to warn consumers about the alleged cancer risks of its weedkiller Roundup on its label.

item.title: Cassidy says he passed a note to Witkoff during heated Trump meeting
item.description: Sen. Bill Cassidy said that after his exchange with the president, he passed a note to Steve Witkoff, saying he would consider changing his war powers vote.

item.title: NATO chief faces Trump's ire in meeting over Iran
item.description: President Trump criticized European allies for not supporting the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran during a meeting with NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte. CBS News' Aaron Navarro has more.

item.title: Are deadly heat waves driving Europeans to embrace air conditioning?
item.description: Only 20% European homes have AC, compared to 90% in the U.S., but as the climate changes, that vast gulf may be set to shrink.

item.title: Taylor Swift wedding venue speculation fueled by MSG permit
item.description: There appear to be new clues about the location of Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding.

item.title: Judge blocks Trump executive order on mail-in voting
item.description: President Trump signed an executive order in March requiring the creation of a list of U.S. citizens eligible to vote in each state and imposing stricter mail-in ballot rules.

item.title: Supreme Court lets Trump strip deportation protections from Syrians, Haitians
item.description: The Supreme Court on Thursday said the Trump administration can move forward with its efforts to strip more than 356,000 Syrian and Haitian immigrants of temporary protections.

item.title: "Excuse My Grandma" hosts on how the podcast has influenced their relationship and perspectives
item.description: Kim Murstein and her grandma Gail Gudnick, the hosts of the popular podcast "Excuse My Grandma," tell "CBS Mornings" how the pandemic helped launch their podcast, its impact on their relationship and what they've learned from each other.

item.title: California officials use license plate readers, drones to crack down on porch pirates
item.description: To stop porch pirates, Irvine police have been using aerial drones to follow suspects from residential areas and shopping malls.

item.title: The hunt for Cambodia's stolen Khmer jewels
item.description: Crowns and precious jewels believed to have been worn by ancient Khmer royalty were tracked down to an unlikely place: a pub parking lot outside London.

item.title: T.J. Miller arrested for allegedly calling in fake bomb threat
item.description: Former "Silicon Valley" star T.J. Miller was arrested Monday night after allegedly calling in a false bomb threat aboard an Amtrak train
